questions.q: According to a recent interview, what is Barnes & Noble CEO James Daunt's stance on selling AI-written books in their stores?
options: He supports selling them if they are clearly labeled and there is public demand.
He wants to ban all books containing any AI-generated text from physical shelves.
He plans to replace self-published authors with Barnes & Noble's own AI models.
He believes AI books should only be sold digitally, not in print.
questions.correctText: He supports selling them if they are clearly labeled and there is public demand.

questions.q: In an experiment highlighted by Vox's 'Today, Explained' podcast, author Vauhini Vara tested whether which group could distinguish her writing from an AI imitation?
Researchers in Ireland recently tracked down a forgotten medieval book in a Roman library that hides the oldest surviving English poem, known as what?
The tiny Los Angeles bookstore 'A Good Used Book' is gaining community popularity for transforming its space every Sunday into what event?
Doireann Ní Ghríofa's newly reviewed book 'Said the Dead' explores the lives of forgotten patients at which historical psychiatric facility in Ireland?
